step.title: Orient on Current State
step.goal: Before formulating new questions, take stock of what is currently known and what is unknown. Identify the highest-value gap to close THIS turn. This step is the loop's re-entry point — every turn restarts here.

substep.title: Inventory the Known
substep.action:
List what is currently established, from the bundle and any accumulated session findings: - Scope facts (restatement, primary tag, slug) - Glossary terms resolved in CONTEXT.md - Research findings recorded in the research file - Decisions locked in linked DRs - Edge cases identified in prior turns - User stances from prior Q&A entries
Be terse. One line per fact. The point is to identify what does NOT need re-litigating, not to recite a transcript.

substep.title: Inventory the Unknown
substep.action:
Enumerate the gaps. Categories: - Scoping gaps (what the work is supposed to cover; what it is NOT) - Codebase facts (how the system currently behaves; what exists) - Domain facts (how similar systems handle similar problems) - Decision points (alternatives with real trade-offs that need a chosen path) - Edge cases (deletion behavior, error states, permissions, concurrency) - Constraints (deadlines, regulatory, compatibility) - Term ambiguity (words used by the user that need glossary precision)
Be honest. If a gap is "we have not even thought about X yet", surface it. Suppressed unknowns become surprises during ticket-design or implementation.

substep.title: Pick the Highest-Value Gap
substep.action:
Select ONE gap to close this turn. Heuristics: - Closest to scope > deeper detail (scope first) - Blocks other gaps > stands alone (unblockers first) - Single-question shape > multi-pronged (cheaper first, unless a multi-prong is genuinely required) - User-answerable now > research-bound (don't burn subagent calls when the user can answer immediately)
Store as {{session.turn_focus}}: a one-sentence description of the gap being closed this turn.

substep.title: Declare Mode for This Turn
substep.action:
Q↔R has four sub-activities (QRDSPIV §Q↔R). Declare which the current turn will primarily use:
- **Brainstorming**: vague idea -> concrete possibilities. Use when the user's request is fuzzy and needs shape before research can target it. - **Research**: factual gathering. Use when the gap is "what exists" or "what do others do". - **Elicitation**: targeted question to the user. Use when only the user can answer (preferences, scoping decisions, business constraints). - **Prototyping**: throwaway runnable artifact (LOGIC or UI). Use when the gap is "I do not know if this is feasible" or "I do not know what this would feel like".
Most turns will use one mode dominantly; some may blend two. Store as {{session.turn_mode}}.

substep.title: Display Turn Plan

section.title: One gap per turn
section.description: Multi-gap turns blur subagent dispatch and dilute the user's attention at the per-turn checkpoint. One focused gap per turn makes the Q&A log readable and the readiness gate evaluable. If two gaps are genuinely entangled (cannot answer one without the other), pick the more upstream one and let the second emerge from its resolution.

section.title: Hide-the-intent does NOT apply here
section.description: Spec §3 and QRDSPIV note that "hide the intent" applies only to implementation Q↔R (where the agent gathers codebase facts WITHOUT reading what the spec says to change, to prevent premature opinion-forming). At the ticket-writing layer the agent is open about what is being grilled — the user is a participant in the grilling, not a subject of it. State the focus plainly; do not sandbag.

section.title: When the bundle is already rich
section.description: On later turns, the bundle has accumulated research findings, resolved Q&A entries, and possibly DRs. The "known" inventory in 2a compresses these into a one-line-per-fact summary. The point is not to re-prove what is already on disk; the point is to choose what to investigate NEXT.

section.title: If the unknown inventory is empty
section.description: If 2b produces no genuine unknowns AND step 6's readiness criteria are all clearly met, the loop is converging. In that case the next turn IS the readiness gate — skip 2c/2d and proceed straight to step 6 to confirm and exit. But: do not force this early. The most common failure mode is exiting Q↔R too soon, not too late.
